title: Add installation of pre-commit to Makefile
body:
pre-commit is required for running certain tests by the Makefile.
Adding pre-commit to install.tools allows users to easily use
and test podman.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: Oh dear. That CI failure does not look easily solved.
@cevich How do I get python3-pip installed into the Cirrus VMs?

In Chris's absence, I'll chime in but possibly unhelpfully: instead of jumping through these hoops, would it make sense instead to leave `.install.pre-commit` out of `.install.tools`, and just change the lint error message to tell users to run that target?

@mheon @baude @vrothberg @giuseppe @QiWang19 @jwhonce @TomSweeneyRedHat Can we get this one in please. Ready to merge.

> In Chris's absence, I'll chime in but possibly unhelpfully: instead of jumping through these hoops, would

Thanks @edsantiago ya, I'm nervous of using `pip` in CI, since it bypasses the native package system.  Unless it's run inside a python virtualenv, using `pip` system-wide is a "no-no" in my book.  It can really confuse all questions of "which code am I running"?
